meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit for Students: Ship Faster and Finish Projects
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning your SaaS projects. Use this intermediate guide to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to build, ship, and launch your first real-world app.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' cycle stems from over-engineering the boilerplate. By utilizing a standardized SaaS starter kit, you offload authentication, database schemas, and billing setup. Integrating Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for iterative implementation allows you to focus purely on core business logic, ensuring you ship functional products consistently.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned GitHub repositories is paved with good intentions and broken 'perfect' architectures. You start with a vision, code for three days, get stuck on OAuth implementation or database migrations, and the momentum dies. It is not a failure of your intelligence; it is a failure of your workflow.

To break this cycle, you must stop building the foundation from scratch. A professional SaaS starter kit acts as your skeleton, allowing you to focus on the muscle—your unique value proposition. By pairing this with AI-powered development tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform from a frustrated student into a high-velocity product engineer.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Initialize your project using a battle-tested SaaS starter to handle the heavy lifting of auth and billing immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular SaaS architecture for a Next.js project including Supabase auth and Stripe integration patterns.

steps.title: Define Domain Schema
steps.description: Use AI to map your business logic to database entities without getting lost in SQL syntax errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a task management application with user relationships, subscription tiers, and audit logs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file schema.prisma --prompt 'Add a table for user usage metrics tracking'

steps.title: Implement Core API Routes
steps.description: Focus on building the CRUD operations that actually matter to your users, leaving the boilerplate to your starter kit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Next.js API route that validates user subscription status before allowing data mutation.

steps.title: UI Component Orchestration
steps.description: Build the interface using pre-built library components to maintain design consistency without manual CSS hacking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive dashboard layout using Tailwind CSS and Radix UI components that matches the SaaS starter theme.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --dir /components --prompt 'Implement dark mode toggle using standard theme context'

steps.title: Automated Testing & QA
steps.description: Before calling it finished, ensure your core features work by generating unit tests for your critical path.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Vitest unit tests for the authentication middleware and the main task creation workflow.

pro_tips: Never write an authentication hook from scratch; always treat auth as a plug-and-play middleware service.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it; 'self-documenting' is a myth, but AI-generated docs are a reality.
If you find yourself spending more than 2 hours on a single CSS bug, pivot to a different component and return with fresh eyes.
Prioritize 'feature toggles' so you can ship the app even if certain modules are incomplete.
Your first project should be limited to 3 main features; cut everything else to ensure you reach the 'Deploy' button.
content.next_steps: Once your first MVP is deployed, document your learning process in a blog post and share it with the AI Builder community to get feedback on your architecture.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the roadmap; now you need the discipline to cross the finish line. Stop dreaming about your SaaS and start executing the build. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
